hops.context: When the HV executes, it has to perform a set of macro-tasks
Loading the next VM state according to the VM scheduler

question: In the context of real-time virtualization, when a hypervisor (HV) is executing and performing macro tasks, what specific action involves reprogramming the hardware physical memory protection (hPMP)?

options.A: Updating the hypervisor's own internal state for task management
options.B: Loading the next virtual machine (VM) state according to the VM scheduler
options.C: Synchronizing the physical memory access permissions with external devices
options.D: Adjusting the virtual CPUs allocation among different VMs

explanation: Reprogramming the hardware physical memory protection (hPMP) is specifically involved when loading the next VM state as per the VM scheduler. This is necessary to ensure that the memory protection settings align with the new VM state's requirements.
context: When the HV executes, it has to perform a set of macro-tasks | Loading the next VM state according to the VM scheduler | reprogramming hPMP
